description:
You are the on-call Site Reliability Engineer.  
  The operations playbook says that, at the top of every hour, you must record a one-shot
  “uptime benchmark” for three critical network endpoints.

  What is already on the system
  --------------------------------
  1. A text file that enumerates the endpoints you have to check:
        /home/user/services.list

     Its exact contents are three lines, in this order (no blank lines, no extra
     whitespace):
        127.0.0.1
        192.0.2.1
        203.0.113.1

  2. No log directory exists yet under /home/user/.  
     You have permission to create anything inside /home/user.

  Your task
  ---------
  Using **one single shell command** (it may be a pipeline and may contain subshells,
  semicolons, etc., but the entire action must be typed and submitted as a
  single line in the terminal), do the following:

  1. Create a new directory called
        /home/user/logs
     if it does not already exist.

  2. Ping each endpoint **exactly once** (ICMP echo, one packet, 1-second timeout).
     Any standard Linux utility is acceptable (`ping`, `fping`, etc.); just make sure
     you attempt one packet per host.

  3. Generate the log file
        /home/user/logs/uptime_report.log
     and **overwrite** it if it is already present.

  Required log-file format
  ------------------------
  • The file must consist of **exactly three lines**, one for each address and in the
    same order as they appear in /home/user/services.list.

  • Each line must have the form

        <address><single space><status>

    where  
      – <address> is the literal text from services.list, and  
      – <status> is the uppercase word  
            UP      if the ping command exited with return-code 0  
            DOWN    for any non-zero return-code.

  • No leading or trailing whitespace on any line.  
  • A single trailing newline (ASCII 0x0A) after the last line is mandatory.  
  • Do not create or modify any other files.

  Example of the **exact** expected contents, given the three addresses already in
  services.list and typical connectivity inside the training container:

        127.0.0.1 UP
        192.0.2.1 DOWN
        203.0.113.1 DOWN

  After you run your one-liner, you can use `cat /home/user/logs/uptime_report.log`
  to manually verify that the file looks exactly as specified.

  Hint (do **not** paste this directly): tools like `awk`, `xargs`, or a small
  one-line `while read` loop are perfectly capable of performing this task in a
  single-command invocation.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth verification data that the automated grading harness will use.
    These values MUST NOT appear in the public task description.
  -->

  <!-- Pre-existing input file -->
  <file path="/home/user/services.list" must_exist="yes"><![CDATA[
127.0.0.1
192.0.2.1
203.0.113.1
]]></file>

  <!-- Expected artefacts after the agent finishes -->
  <expected_log path="/home/user/logs/uptime_report.log"><![CDATA[
127.0.0.1 UP
192.0.2.1 DOWN
203.0.113.1 DOWN
]]></expected_log>

  <!-- Directories that must exist -->
  <dir path="/home/user/logs" />
